name: Pork Stew with Beer
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the fat from the pork.
2. Cut the pork into 1-inch cubes.
3. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven until hot.
4. Cook the pork over medium heat, stirring frequently, until all liquid is evaporated and the pork is brown, about 25 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon.
5. Drain all but 2 tablespoons of fat from the Dutch oven.
6. Cook and stir the onion and garlic in the Dutch oven until the onion is tender.
7. Add the tomatoes, chile, cilantro, salt, cumin, and oregano. Break up the tomatoes with a fork.
8. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat.
9. Simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes.
10. Stir in the pork and beer. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat.
11. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes.
12. Stir in the red pepper. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat.
13. Simmer, uncovered, until the pork is tender and the sauce is thickened, about 15 minutes.
14. Skim off any excess fat.
15. Serve with rice.
